prompt: A new request arrived and the scope is unclear. Choose the track, active hub, next skill, and update workflow-state.
One active request already has routing and state. Drive the next solid handoff without reopening planning.
This work is larger than quick-flow and planning artifacts are stale. Use the planning hub to sequence analyst, PM, architect, and story slicing without losing context.
The story is approved and planning is ready. Change code with controlled scope, test evidence, and update workflow-state.
Implementation exists after a risky refactor and confidence is low. Coordinate verification and collect regression evidence.
Work is about to be called done. Check acceptance criteria, risk, regression scope, and write a QA report.
Before deploy, produce a readiness verdict with smoke signals, rollback guardrails, and release evidence.
A naming cutover might leave stale compatibility tokens behind. Scan source and runtime files with an allowlist gate.
The chat is getting long and may resume in a new thread. Create checkpoint, rehydrate context, handoff, and diff continuity.
The agent is about to say fixed and ready. Run one last evidence pass before making the completion claim.
List completion claims and map each claim to fresh proof output without deciding overall readiness.
Implementation evidence exists. Produce a formal go or no-go readiness verdict against acceptance criteria, risk, regression scope, and qa-report.
Use api-integration for network-facing code. We are changing API clients, webhooks, auth, retries, timeouts, and error mapping for an external service.
Use data-persistence before touching schemas, repositories, migrations, transactions, caches, and consistency boundaries.
Use dependency-management because a package is being added or upgraded and lockfiles, version pinning, toolchain, and CI drift must be checked.
Use accessibility-review before completion claims for frontend work. Check keyboard navigation, visible focus, semantic structure, labels, contrast, and hold verdict evidence.
Use policy-guard for high-risk agent operations. Scan shell commands, path traversal, hard-coded secrets, prompt-injection phrases, broad allowlists, and fail closed.
Use impact-radar before touching runtime, adapters, templates, scripts, docs, and packaging so blast radius and verification gates are explicit.

Need project-architecture support, not architect planning. Analyze existing codebase shape, entry points, execution flow, dependency direction, architecture drift, hotspots, and update the living architecture reference.
Use ux-structure when a user-facing flow needs sharper information hierarchy, cleaner interaction flow, loading empty error states, and less generic AI-looking UI.
